item: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.
notes: Both agents oppose automatic priority for society over individual rights from their opening turns through the end. Agent A concedes only narrow clear-harm limits and repeatedly warns about public-good language harming minorities or dissenters. Agent B closely mirrors that rights-as-guardrail frame in Indonesian, allowing only narrow exceptions for direct harm. No visible flip or directional drift is observable in the text.
